title: quercus × atlantica
intro: Quercus × atlantica, also known as the Atlas oak, is a hybrid species of deciduous oak tree, resulting from the cross between Quercus faginea and Quercus suber. The bark and leaves of the Atlas oak have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their rich content of tannins and polyphenols, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus × atlantica have been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The bark and leaves of the Atlas oak can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
